expectations.text: The OWASP-style jti denylist revocation model is not reported as a failure
expectations.evidence: The denylist is credited in 'What is done well' as store-backed validation that fails closed; never flagged. Lane discipline held

expectations.text: Report acknowledges rotation and reuse detection with family revocation are present and correct
expectations.evidence: Called 'textbook': rotation burns old jti before minting, family-wide revocation on reuse with logging (R04, R09, R10 credited)

expectations.text: Report notes at least one real Tier 3 gap (refresh cookie path scope or rate limiting)
expectations.evidence: Both found: T25 path '/' ADVISORY and R13 missing rate limiting as FAIL (PLAUSIBLE) with the upstream-gateway assumption named

expectations.text: Overall grade is A or B
expectations.evidence: INVALIDATED BY FIXTURE BUG: the report graded C on a CONFIRMED R12 failure that is genuinely present in the fixture (password change revokes only the presented cookie's family; sessions from other logins survive). The fixture author intended the code to be correct here and it is not. The auditor was right; the ground truth was wrong

expectations.text: No finding is reported as a confirmed failure that is actually a false positive
expectations.evidence: The single FAIL (CONFIRMED), R12, is a true positive verified by reading the fixture: families are minted per login (routes.js:33) and only the presented family is revoked (routes.js:91-104). Zero false positives

score: 4/4 valid (1 assertion void: fixture ground truth was wrong and the skill caught the real bug)
